signals.headline: Congress and IUML finalize seat-sharing: Congress to contest 95 seats, IUML 27 seats in 140-seat Kerala Assembly electio
signals.raw:
Congress and IUML finalize seat-sharing: Congress to contest 95 seats, IUML 27 seats in 140-seat Kerala Assembly elections** | YES | HIGH confidence[5]

This agreement resolves ongoing IUML demands for a larger share (pushing for 30 seats based on local body poll performance)[2], slightly above their 2021 allocation of 25 (where they won 15)[2], and aligns with earlier promises of 27[2]. No newer

signals.headline: On April 9, Bitcoin rose ~4
signals.raw: On April 9, Bitcoin rose ~4.5% to $71,362 after a two-week U.S.-Iran ceasefire announcement crashed oil prices from $112/barrel, triggering $427M in short liquidations and shifting Fed rate cut expectations.[1]
matched_markets: Bitcoin Up or Down - April 17, 12:00PM-12:15PM ET

signals.headline: By April 14-15, BTC hit a one-month high near $75,000 (e
signals.raw: By April 14-15, BTC hit a one-month high near $75,000 (e.g., $74,286 on Apr 15), driven by ETF inflows ($471M on Apr 6), institutional demand, and Kraken's $200M investment from Deutsche Börse, with predictions targeting $75K-$80K if momentum holds.[1][2][3][4]

signals.headline: Confidence: LOW** - Search results lack updates since Apr 15; no "just confirmed" events today to impact prediction mark
signals.raw: Confidence: LOW** - Search results lack updates since Apr 15; no "just confirmed" events today to impact prediction markets like geopolitical resolutions or policy shifts.[1][2][3][4][5]

signals.headline: South Korea's annual inflation rose to 2
signals.raw: South Korea's annual inflation rose to 2.2% in March 2026, up from 2.0% in prior months but below 2.4% forecasts.**[1]
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: HEADLINE | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
HEADLINE | YES | HIGH confidence**  
This March figure, sourced from Statistics Korea via Trading Economics and last updated post-release, marks the most recent confirmed data amid Middle East energy shocks pushing prices higher, with Bank of Korea holding rates at 2.5% (7th meeting) and raising its 2026 inflation outlook to 2.2%.[1][4] No newer April announcements confirmed as of now; this uptick
